title: My Life — Volume 1
author: Wagner, Richard
summary: Richard Wagner recounts his early life from his 1813 birth in Leipzig through childhood in Dresden. His father, Friedrich, a police clerk who admired theatre, died when Richard was an infant. The actor Ludwig Geyer became his stepfather, married his mother, and nurtured Wagner’s exposure to theatre and painting. Wagner describes school at Possendorf with the vicar Wetzel, his fascination with Mozart and Greek independence, childish theatrical roles including a tableau vivant, and the family’s poverty after his father’s death. Episodes include his fragile childhood health, his father’s dying blessing, time in Eisleben with a grandmother, and returning to Dresden.

characters.personality: curious, imaginative, attracted to theatre and music

characters.lifespan: Richard’s biological father; police clerk; died in October 1813

characters.name: Ludwig Geyer
characters.lifespan: Actor and painter who married Wagner’s mother after Friedrich’s death; raised Richard and siblings; later exhausted by exertions

characters.name: Wagner's mother
characters.lifespan: Widow of Friedrich; married Ludwig Geyer; cared for the family and children

characters.lifespan: Lived in Eisleben; died shortly after Friedrich’s death

characters.name: Wetzel

characters.name: C. M. von Weber
